# Daxchain

> Daxchain is a small blockchain lab building practical Ethereum tooling and experimental tokens. Every project is open source on GitHub under the daxchain-io organization.

Daxchain favors composable, agent-friendly, command-line-first tools for working with EVM chains. The website at https://daxchain.io is a single static page that links to the project repositories.

## Projects

- [EVM Tools](https://github.com/daxchain-io/evm-tools): A suite of composable command-line tools for observing EVM chains, written in Go. Producers (`evm-stream`, `evm-balance`) emit newline-delimited JSON on stdout; sinks (`evm-sink-kafka`, `evm-sink-webhook`, `evm-sink-file`) consume that stream and forward it to Kafka, webhooks, or rotating local files, at-least-once.
- [Daxie](https://github.com/daxchain-io/daxie): An agent-first Ethereum wallet with local custody, JSON-first command-line workflows, MCP support, and policy guardrails for automated spend.
- [NZT-48](https://github.com/daxchain-io/nzt48): An experimental ERC-20 where untouched addresses appear to have a balance, built to probe wallet, indexer, and contract assumptions.

## Links

- [GitHub organization](https://github.com/daxchain-io)
- [Website source](https://github.com/daxchain-io/daxchain-io.github.io)
